commands.name: sfuzz

arguments.description: Silent output mode (generally for CLI fuzzing)
arguments.raw: -q	 Silent output mode (generally for CLI fuzzing)

arguments.description: Begin fuzzing at the test specified.
arguments.raw: -b	 Begin fuzzing at the test specified.

arguments.description: Wait time for reading the socket
arguments.raw: -t	 Wait time for reading the socket

arguments.description: Create a new logfile after each fuzz
arguments.raw: -n	 Create a new logfile after each fuzz

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# sfuzz -h
 		Simple Fuzzer
 By:	 Aaron Conole
 version: 0.7.0
 url:	 http://aconole.brad-x.com/programs/sfuzz.html
 EMAIL:	 apconole@yahoo.com
 Build-prefix: /usr
 	-h	 This message.
 	-V	 Version information.
 
 networking / output:
 	-v	 Verbose output
 	-q	 Silent output mode (generally for CLI fuzzing)
 	-X	 prints the output in hex
 
 	-b	 Begin fuzzing at the test specified.
 	-e	 End testing on failure.
 	-t	 Wait time for reading the socket
 	-S	 Remote host
 	-p	 Port
 	-T|-U|-O TCP|UDP|Output mode
 	-R	 Refrain from closing connections (ie: "leak" them)
 
 	-f	 Config File
 	-L	 Log file
 	-n	 Create a new logfile after each fuzz
 	-r	 Trim the tailing newline
 	-D	 Define a symbol and value (X=y).
 	-l	 Only perform literal fuzzing
 	-s	 Only perform sequence fuzzing

examples.command:
sfuzz -S 192.168.1.1 -p 10443 -T -f /usr/share/sfuzz/sfuzz-sample/basic.http
[12:53:47] dumping options:
    filename: </usr/share/sfuzz/sfuzz-sample/basic.http>
    state:    <8>
    lineno:   <56>
    literals:  [74]
    sequences: [34]
    symbols: [0]
    req_del:  <200>
    mseq_len: <10024>
    plugin: <none>
    s_syms: <0>
    literal[1] = [AREALLYBADSTRING]

apt_metadata.description:
Black Box testing utilities
In the same vein as the Generic Protocol Framework, sfuzz is
a really simple to use black box testing suite called Simple
Fuzzer (what else would you expect?). The goal is to provide
a simple to use, but fairly powerful and flexible black box
testing utility.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use sfo because ### sfuzz.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair sfo with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `sfo --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### sfuzz.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install sfuzz
Install with: sudo apt install sfuzz
